---
title: "Jobs"
---

The job safety system reports races as type errors that read like nonsense until
you know the shape. Each gotcha here is a race the safety system caught, with the
form that makes it safe.

## Parallel scheduling needs IJobFor

Under Unity 6, `ScheduleParallel()` requires {{< unity-type IJobFor >}}.
`IJobParallelFor` does not extend it. Use `IJobFor` for all parallel jobs.

## Cross-job type tracking loss

An `IJobEntity` source-generates a `ComponentTypeHandle<T>` for every `ref` and
`in` parameter. When a non-`IJobEntity` job overwrites `state.Dependency`, the
safety system reports type `T` "not assigned to Dependency." The fix is to stop
using the tracked handle and use an unsafe lookup.

```{.wrong}
partial struct Move : IJobEntity {
    public ComponentLookup<Velocity> Vel;
    void Execute(ref Position p, in Entity e) { p.Value += Vel[e].Value; }
}
```

```{.right}
partial struct Move : IJobEntity {
    public BovineLabs.Core.Iterators.UnsafeComponentLookup<Velocity> Vel;
    void Execute(ref Position p, in Entity e) { p.Value += Vel[e].Value; }
}
```

## The aliasing trap

A `ref T` parameter and a `ComponentLookup<T>` field on the *same* `IJobEntity`
throw `two containers may not be the same`. Either drop the manual lookup and use
only parameters, or switch to {{< unity-type UnsafeComponentLookup >}}.

## Parallel buffer writes

`BufferLookup<T>` does not support parallel writing. Use
{{< unity-type UnsafeBufferLookup >}} with `[NativeDisableParallelForRestriction]`.

::: {.gotcha title="Instance NativeList fields are shared across invocations"}
An instance `NativeList` or `NativeArray` field on a parallel job is one
container shared by every parallel invocation — a data race. Allocate inside
`Execute` with `Allocator.Temp`, which is thread-local and fast.
:::

```{.right}
public void Execute(int index) {
    var local = new Unity.Collections.NativeList<int>(8, Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp);
    local.Dispose();
}
```

## SetComponentEnabled in parallel

`SetComponentEnabled` flips a chunk-level bitmask; two entities in the same chunk
race. Record the change through an {{< unity-type EntityCommandBuffer >}}
`ParallelWriter` instead, keyed by `entryIndex` — never `jobIndex`, which can
overlap across threads.

## Recursion is not supported in Burst

Rewrite recursion as an iterative walk over an explicit stack.

```{.right}
var stack = new Unity.Collections.NativeList<Entity>(8, Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp);
stack.Add(root);
while (stack.Length > 0) {
    var current = stack[stack.Length - 1];
    stack.RemoveAtSwapBack(stack.Length - 1);
    Process(current);
}
stack.Dispose();
```

## Reading a singleton after scheduling its writer

`GetSingleton<T>()` on the main thread races with a job still writing `T`.
Complete the dependency before any main-thread component read. The same applies
to `SetComponent` after a `map.Build(...)` and to clearing a collection a prior
job still reads.

```{.right}
state.Dependency.Complete();
var data = SystemAPI.GetSingleton<Config>();
```
